# Transcrição: Should_you_quit_university_to_pursue_your_passion_

## [00:00 - 01:22]

You obviously had a lot of different career changes. When you make the change, did you have any doubts? So an example I can think of, quitting university. I was going to quit but I was like maybe I should just differ or maybe I should just do one subject. But then my mum pulled me aside and this is not advice for you. This was the advice my mum gave me because my mum knew me. I don't know you that well so it's irresponsible for me to give you direct advice. But what my mum said to me was my mum said, I know you son, if you've got a backup you won't give it your all. Don't tell your dad this. Quit. Because if you don't then you get the degree. Your dad's going to be really upset and you're not going to count it. And my mum was right because because I quit I was then able to give my all. Because doubts make you wobbly. Doubts make you kind of go, oh I don't know should I go back, should I go there? And there's something powerful about boldness. You make everyone else feel calm and everyone else doesn't doubt you. Because and when you're bold you doubt yourself less. Me, not you, me. I'd go all in and if I stuff up then I'll doubt myself and worry and gop come up with a backup plan. I just always thought what's the worst case scenario? I become an accountant and make 55, 58 grand a year. Is it that bad? So it never was that bad. So when I looked at the situation for what it really was I never really got scared. But just always think of what's the worst case scenario. If you're already going to do something why half-ass it? Again I believe in the full ass philosophy and just go for it.
